steps.title: Rewrite the exponent
steps.content:
The choices all use $a^{56}$ inside a radical, so rewrite the original exponent with numerator $56$. Multiplying the numerator and denominator by the same number keeps the exponent equal to the original value.
$$\frac{7}{8}=\frac{7\cdot 8}{8\cdot 8}=\frac{56}{64}$$

steps.title: Convert to radical form
steps.content:
A fractional exponent can be written as a radical: $a^{m/n}$ is the $n$th root of $a^m$. With denominator $64$ and numerator $56$, the expression becomes a $64$th root.
$$a^{7/8}=a^{56/64}=\sqrt[64]{a^{56}}$$
The answer is B.
